name: kaminblocking
description: This psychological task investigates the [Kamin blocking phenomenon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blocking_effect), where people learn that (1) cue A predicts an outcome, then that (2) cue A and cue B predicts an outcome. When asked if B alone predicts the outcome, they show a diminished response. A is a already a good predictor of the outcome; the intuition is: 'no need to learn from B, given that A already predicts the outcome.' In short, A blocks B.
